mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months following a rate cut, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in >60% of instances where UNRATE rises >0.2pp, or signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized strength in consumer demand and residential investment, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative forward returns in >50% of triggered months, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically indistinguishable from zero in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative 5-day returns in >55% of observations during early-cycle expansions, or the predictive alpha decays to zero within 3 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the consumer price index exceeding 0.4 percent triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of months following CPI spikes >0.4%, or the signal fails to predict direction with accuracy above 50% in a walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a decline in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market expansion, supporting corporate earnings expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative forward returns in >55% of triggered months, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects broad economic expansion and rising corporate revenues, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >50% of quarters following positive GDP revisions, or the Rank IC falls below 0.01 over rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 0.3 percent signals contracting manufacturing output and weakening supply-side fundamentals, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in >55% of months following industrial production declines >0.3%, or the signal accuracy drops below 50% in walk-forward validation.
